draft.introduction: Matthew 7:24–29 presents the parable of the wise and foolish builders, the final image of the Sermon on the Mount. From Codex Sinaiticus, fourth century.

translation.text: Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house upon the rock. And the rain came down, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it did not fall, for it had been founded upon the rock.
translation.original_text: πας ουν οστις ακουει μου τους λογους τουτους και ποιει αυτους

translation.text: And it came to pass when Jesus finished these words, the crowds were astonished at his teaching, for he was teaching them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
translation.original_text: και εγενετο οτε ετελεσεν ο ιησους τους λογους τουτους

translator_notes.note: The Greek phronimos (φρόνιμος), rendered “wise,” denotes practical wisdom rather than theoretical knowledge. The parable insists that hearing without doing is equivalent to building on sand.
